observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the GSO to reconcile and check their property records pursuant to Section C3, Chapter V of the Manual on Property Custodianship.

Further, we recommended that Management require the GSO to maintain Property Cards and the Municipal Accountant to maintain an Equipment Ledger Cards so that reconciliation be made periodically and any unreconciled differences be adjusted easily.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to present the current and non-current portions of the liability as separate classifications on the face of the Statement of Financial Position in accordance with paragraph 80 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to release the retention money to the concerned contractors/suppliers pursuant to Section 62.1 and 6.1 and 6.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in which the contractor shall  make a request to the procuring entity for the release of the retention money and submit a security in the form of cash, bank guarantee, irrevocable standby letter of credit from a commercial bank, GSIS or surety bond callable on demand equivalent for the retention money substituted for.

Further, we recommended that Management instruct the end-users to attach a certification that the projects were completed and inspected.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to analyze the payable accounts to ensure the accuracy of the balances in the financial statements and only record transactions/set up liability based on valid obligations with complete evidences or documents, and revert the balances of Accounts Payable that have been outstanding for more than two years which are undocumented and no longer valid to the Unappropriated Surplus pursuant to COA Circular No. 99-004.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly or appropriately classify and record the expenses in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts corresponding with the specific purpose/s of the expenditure/s for which it was created to present fairly the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to intensify the immediate collections of the account dues from the concerned officers and employees through salary deductions from the general payroll;
Inform concerned officers and employees that failure on their part to settle their accounts shall constitute a ground for the withholding of their salaries, and
Strictly observe the provisions of Malversation or Misappropriation of government funds under Article 217 and Article 220 of the Revised Penal Code.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately comply and submit to the Office of the Auditor the necessary lacking supporting documents as mentioned above pursuant to Section 4 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management instruct the GSO to prepare and submit the required Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Driver’s Trip Ticket in every official travel pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 as basis in determining the reasonableness of fuel consumption in order to control any wasteful, excessive and unnecessary consumption of gasoline.

We recommended and Management agreed to designate proper special disbursing officers or employees for other legally purpose for which the expenditures were created.

Further, we recommended and Management also agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop the granting of cash advances for the implementation of feeding program or other projects and programs for specific legal purpose.  Purchases of needed goods for the implementation of the programs are to be paid by checks directly to the supplier for purposes of the creditable tax required to be withheld on income and be remitted to the BIR.

We recommended that Management instruct the:
Bids and Awards Committee to coordinate with heads of implementing offices/units to accomplish completely and properly the information in the prescribed forms specifically on the date portions for every procurement activity, and to be vigilant in accomplishing the prescribed forms to ascertain the authenticity of the transactions and to promote operational efficiency and effectiveness of the Municipality; and
Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Inspection and Acceptance Committee and the GSO to require the Suppliers to indicate the date the goods were delivered and the date the payment was received to determine that the terms and conditions were in accordance with the contract.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe and deduct the employees’ obligations due the GSIS, which shall be given preference over the claims of Private Lending Institutions, pursuant to Section 47 of GAA FY 2020 in order to forestall the imposition of sanctions spelled out in Sections 7 and 52 of RA No. 8291 for those officials who failed to deduct the applicable GSIS loan amortizations from employees’ salaries.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly supervise the personnel assigned in the Treasury Office and instruct them to avoid erasures or alterations in the preparation of checks to minimize wastage or misuse of accountable forms and to strengthen internal control system.

We recommended that Management through the I-Plan Head and I-Build Head to serve/issue the Contract and the Notice to Proceed immediately to the Contractor as the new sites have long been approved, in order to hasten the construction of the two unimplemented solar dryers and to timely deliver the socio-economic benefits to the intended beneficiaries.   Likewise, we recommended that Management instruct the BAC to require the Contractor to submit the updated legal documents.

We recommended that Management require all officers and employees with outstanding cash advances to account and settle immediately after the purpose for which these were granted in conformity with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; Section 89 of PD No. 1445; immediately issue demand letters to those employees who are no longer in the service with unsettled/ unliquidated cash advances; refrain from granting additional cash advances to officers and employees with existing cash advances unless the previous cash advances are first settled; and cause the refund of any unused/unspent/unutilized cash advances and/or no longer needed to the collecting officer to be receipted and shall be deposited to the authorized government depository bank.

1. Require the Accountant to determine the cause/causes of the reconciling difference of P194,449.14 and make the necessary adjustments.
2. Inform the bank that the BIR remittances of the municipality for the months of September and December 2015 were deducted twice in the municipality's account.
3. Prepare adjusting entries to correct the deficiencies noted.

A letter has been served to the bank but they informed management to refer the matter to the Bureau of Internal Revenue for it is already remitted to the said agency. The necessary adjustments were properly taken up.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer enjoin all officials and employees of the municipality to comply with the requirements set forth under Section 48 of the General Appropriations Act and adopt control measures to ensure that the employee’s monthly net take home pay shall not be lower than P3,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The management is complying with the said provision beginning March 2016 payroll. Net take home pay of employees were already P3,000.00 or more since March 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management should comply with the Provisions of RA 7160 which clearly requires Sanggunian ordinance or resolutions prior to project prosecution.
observations.managementAction: The management is taking an appeal related to the disallowance of the payment amounting to P3,751,497.37. However, in its Resolution was succeeding required before the posting of Invitation to Bid.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deduct from the succeeding payroll the amount remitted in favor of the above-mentioned municipal employees in order to adjust the abnormal balance of the account Due to Pag-IBIG.
observations.managementAction: Most of the concerned employees refunded the amount due from them totaling P2,737.46.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund account in the Trust Fund books in compliance with Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The unutilized balance of the LDRRMF had been actually transferred to the Special Trust Fund last October 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant should facilitate the daily recording and timely submission of the monthly accounts in compliance with the above-cited provision in order to give the auditor reasonable time to determine the validity, legality and propriety of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: The management see to it that they were to submit the said documents on the prescribed period of time.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended management to require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to submit copies of perfected contracts within the prescribed period to facilitate review and evaluation of the contracts.
observations.managementAction: The new set of Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) had submitted some copies of contracts and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within 5 days but some were submitted beyond the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the contractors to post the Warranty Securities of the completed infrastructure projects to assure for the protection/guarantee against possible structural defects/failures that may occur during the applicable warranty period.
observations.managementAction: The management could hardly implement such provision but they will try to comply with the said rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management agreed, that agency require the Appraisal Committee in recognizing the value of these properties which will serve as their basis in recording the value of the donated properties.
observations.managementAction: Donated properties particularly those from the DOH were already recorded both in the RPCPPE and books of accounts. The donated properties from a private individual remained unrecorded in the books because no appraisal had been made on the value of these properties.

observations.recommendation: We have recommend that GSO-Designate and Accounting Department work together to reconcile the physical inventory count and accounting record and verify the unaccounted properties. Attention should be given to properties held by individuals no longer connected with the agency, and those which cannot be ascertained on its whereabouts. We further recommended that the Property Acknowledgement Receipt be renewed every three years or as often as necessary.
observations.managementAction: Some properties were located by the GSO-Designate, however these properties were destroyed/unserviceable.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that MPDO prepare an accounting report and recognize the necessary revenue generated from the sale of the soft broom inventory. Also, the Accountant and the MPDO need to reconcile their records regarding the fund used in the soft broom production.
observations.managementAction: No action undertaken.
observations.reason: No report had been submitted by the MPDO.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the refund of the excess amount of Productivity Enhancement Incentive granted to officials and employees. Also, the management should strictly adhere with the provision of EO No. 80 specifically on the authorized amount to be granted for the payment of PEI.
observations.managementAction: A monthly deduction from the 37 payroll of the concerned officials and employees starting November 2016 had been made. As of December 2016 the amount of P131,000.00 refunded. Likewise such disallowance were deducted from the claims of Terminal Leave Pay of the affected officials and employees.
